audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for water at Rephidim/Massah/Meribah, staff/rock, Amalek attack, raised hands, altar YHWH-nissi.

verses.restored: All congregation of children of Israel journeyed from wilderness of Sin by their stages according to mouth of YHWH, and camped at Rephidim; and there was no water for the people to drink.
The people quarreled with Moses and said, Give us water so we may drink. Moses said to them, Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test YHWH?
The people thirsted there for water, and the people grumbled against Moses and said, Why is this, that you brought us up from Egypt to kill me and my children and my livestock with thirst?
Moses cried to YHWH, saying, What shall I do for this people? A little more and they will stone me.
YHWH said to Moses, Pass before the people and take with you some elders of Israel; and your staff with which you struck the Nile, take in your hand and go.
Look, I will stand before you there on the rock at Horeb. You shall strike the rock, and water will come out from it, and the people will drink. Moses did so before eyes of elders of Israel.
He called name of the place Massah and Meribah because of quarrel of children of Israel and because they tested YHWH, saying, Is YHWH in our midst or not?
Then Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
Moses said to Joshua, Choose men for us and go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on top of hill with staff of Divine in my hand.
Joshua did as Moses said to him, to fight with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up top of hill.
It happened when Moses lifted his hand, Israel prevailed; and when he rested his hand, Amalek prevailed.

Hands of Moses were heavy, so they took a stone and placed under him, and he sat on it; Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one from this side and one from that side, and his hands were steady until sun came.
Joshua weakened Amalek and his people by mouth of sword.
YHWH said to Moses, Write this as memorial in the scroll and put in ears of Joshua: I will surely wipe out memory of Amalek from under heavens.
Moses built altar and called its name YHWH-nissi / YHWH is my banner.
He said, Because hand is upon throne/banner of Yah: war for YHWH against Amalek from generation to generation.

translation_notes: Water crisis is real survival fear; do not trivialize Israel's question.
Massah/Meribah name the testing/quarreling memory: Is YHWH among us or not?
Moses' heavy hands require communal support from Aaron and Hur; leadership is not solitary performance.
Amalek material is severe and requires later ethical/reception-history audit.
safety_note: Amalek texts have dangerous reception history. Do not apply Amalek to modern ethnic, religious, or political groups. Here it is preserved as ancient attack-on-the-vulnerable memory and source material requiring careful audit.
